body:
<!--
   PR title (set in the GitHub PR creation form):
     jas0xf_adversarial_neural_representation (0.27)

   Target: commaai/comma_video_compression_challenge  master
   Head:   jas0xf/comma_video_compression_challenge:submission/jas0xf_adversarial_neural_representation

   How to use this file:
     - URLs marked _URL_HERE will be replaced by the real
       https://github.com/jas0xf/... URLs once we push to the fork.
     - The two GIF tags also work as drag-drop placeholders if you'd rather
       have GitHub host them on user-attachments instead of from the fork.
-->

# submission name:
jas0xf_adversarial_neural_representation

# upload zipped `archive.zip`
https://github.com/jas0xf/comma_video_compression_challenge/raw/master/submissions/jas0xf_adversarial_neural_representation/archive.zip

# report.txt
```
=== Evaluation config ===
  batch_size: 16
  device: cuda
  num_threads: 2
  prefetch_queue_depth: 4
  report: submissions/jas0xf_v62_hpac_exp3/report.txt
  seed: 1234
  submission_dir: submissions/jas0xf_v62_hpac_exp3
  uncompressed_dir: /home/runner/work/comma_video_compression_challenge/comma_video_compression_challenge/videos
  video_names_file: /home/runner/work/comma_video_compression_challenge/comma_video_compression_challenge/public_test_video_names.txt
=== Evaluation results over 600 samples ===
  Average PoseNet Distortion: 0.00045701
  Average SegNet Distortion: 0.00067815
  Submission file size: 207,579 bytes
  Original uncompressed size: 37,545,489 bytes
  Compression Rate: 0.00552873
  Final score: 100*segnet_dist + √(10*posenet_dist) + 25*rate = 0.27
```

# does your submission require gpu for evaluation (inflation)?
yes

# did you include the compression script? and want it to be merged?
yes

# additional comments

![demo full](https://github.com/jas0xf/comma_video_compression_challenge/raw/master/submissions/jas0xf_adversarial_neural_representation/assets/demo_full.gif)

![demo slow](https://github.com/jas0xf/comma_video_compression_challenge/raw/master/submissions/jas0xf_adversarial_neural_representation/assets/demo_slow.gif)

Please enjoy my whole month of dedication. The writeup is 24 pages — full recipe, ablations, and the adversarial → neural-representation pivot story: [adversarial neural representation writeup](https://github.com/jas0xf/comma_video_compression_challenge/blob/master/submissions/jas0xf_adversarial_neural_representation/writeup.pdf).

Note: this is a non-Quantizr-style method.

This submission requires `constriction` and `pyppmd` (added to `pyproject.toml`).

commits.messageBody:
Token-based neural codec with master TokenRendererV62 + slave ShrinkSingleNeRV
+ HPAC autoregressive entropy model. 207 KB archive decodes back to a 60-second
1164x874 driving clip in ~22 min on a Tesla T4. Final score: 0.27.

Includes inflate.py + inflate.sh (decoder), compress.py + compress.sh + training/
(full training pipeline with off-the-shelf placeholder hyperparameters during
the active competition window), the validated archive.zip, the eval report, a
24-page writeup, and two demo GIFs in assets/.

Adds constriction + pyppmd to pyproject.toml (used by inflate.py for arithmetic
decoding of tokens.bin and PPMd decompression of hpac.pt.ppmd respectively).

title: jas0xf_adversarial_neural_representation (0.27)
